reasoning: The arithmetic intensity is essentially zero (0.00 FLOP/byte), far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte, and DRAM bandwidth utilization is high at 61.3% of peak, indicating the kernel is saturating memory bandwidth. The scattered writes via the transposed index (`out[transposed & (n-1)]`) produce uncoalesced stores that stress the memory subsystem, while the kernel performs only trivial computation (one FMA per element). The 36.2% long scoreboard stalls are consistent with memory-bound behavior where warps stall waiting for memory operations to complete, but since DRAM utilization is high this reflects genuine bandwidth saturation rather than latency-bound inefficiency.
